hawkish_phrases: the euro area economy could hit a 'speed limit' relatively soon
risks... starting to become more balanced
carefully monitoring potential indirect and second-round effects
wage dynamics would need to be monitored carefully
dovish_phrases: a very substantial degree of monetary accommodation was needed
there were as yet no convincing signs of a more dynamic upward trend in underlying inflation
the Governing Council would look through recent upturns in headline inflation driven by energy prices
still lacking... signs of a convincing upward trend
summary: The Governing Council unanimously held all key interest rates unchanged and confirmed its APP purchase schedule (€80bn monthly until March, then €60bn monthly from April 2017 onwards), with a clear easing bias emphasizing that substantial accommodation remains needed as underlying inflation pressures remain subdued despite recent headline inflation increases driven by energy prices.
